metrics.narrative: The buyer's stated pain is qualitative and dated: 'limited enterprise spend visibility' and 'redundant vendors for the same commodity, so spend is not maximized' (CRM MEDDPICC field, last updated 10 Oct 2024). Around the 2 Apr 2025 intro call, the buying team reiterated wanting one full-suite Source-to-Pay platform instead of point solutions, spanning procurement, sourcing, spend analytics, contracts, supplier management and project/savings management (Next_Step__c narrative). No dollar-quantified impact or ROI figure has been captured from the buyer directly in either call read. Given the buyer's own 9 Jun 2025 comment that a P2P replacement is '2-3 years out,' the urgency behind this pain looks low relative to the 27 Aug 2026 close date.

champion.narrative: Rod Copple was named champion in the CRM MEDDPICC record (10 Oct 2024) as VP of Enterprise Integration and Head of Procurement, but he handed the day-to-day relationship to Cory Griffiths around Q1 2025 (Next_Step__c narrative) and has been largely unresponsive to meeting invites since, per the rep's running log. Cory Griffiths has been the operating-level contact since (attended the 9 Jun 2025 reconnect call, described as 'receptive' but quiet again by 30 Jun 2025), and Brian Lambert was separately consulted on RFP progress on 26 Dec 2025. Neither has demonstrated confirmed access to Dan Lahey or documented internal advocacy, so the champion role reads as developing/weak and is at risk given the repeated quiet stretches from both contacts.

competition.narrative: Salesforce's Competitors__c field lists Coupa, Ivalua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba and Icertis as active competitors, and the 24 Oct 2025 Next Step confirms the buyer was 'still evaluating other vendors with high-level overview demos,' corroborating a multi-vendor evaluation. Neither of the two calls read in this sweep (2 Apr 2025 intro; 11 Sep 2025 demo) contains a verbatim mention of any of these five named rivals, so their real standing versus Zycus is unconfirmed. The most concrete, buyer-voiced competitive fact is the 9 Jun 2025 note that LCS is 'currently using DDSI' for P2P and considering replacement '2-3 years out' -- making the incumbent (DDSI) and plain status-quo inertia the best-evidenced threats right now, ahead of any of the five named point competitors.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's core pain is documented as limited enterprise spend visibility and redundant vendors for the same commodity, meaning spend isn't maximized (CRM MEDDPICC, 10 Oct 2024), and separately the team confirmed on 9 Jun 2025 that they are evaluating a P2P replacement (currently running on DDSI) with S2P capability on their wishlist. That same 9 Jun 2025 note is also the clearest risk signal: the buyer described the P2P replacement timeline as '2-3 years out,' reading as low urgency against a close date 49 days from today (27 Aug 2026). The pain is real and buyer-confirmed, but the buyer's own words suggest low internal urgency right now.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting artifacts, MSA/SOW discussion, or paper-process conversation appears anywhere in the record; the deal has not progressed past Formal Evaluation and the buyer has not yet delivered the RFP that would trigger vendor selection. No forcing function (such as an incumbent contract expiration) has been identified in the data reviewed.

economic_buyer.narrative: Dan Lahey, EVP & CFO, is named as economic buyer and budget owner in the CRM MEDDPICC record (last updated 10 Oct 2024) and is not contradicted by any later evidence, so the role is treated as identified. No call, task, or email in the record shows Dan Lahey ever directly engaging with Zycus; budget sign-off runs entirely through Rod Copple, who reports to Lahey per the same record. Rod Copple has been largely unresponsive since handing day-to-day procurement to Cory Griffiths around Q1 2025 (Next_Step__c narrative), leaving the actual path to the CFO unproven. This is a named-but-unreached economic buyer.

decision_process.narrative: Per the CRM MEDDPICC record (10 Oct 2024), Rod Copple recently took over procurement after 28 years at LCS and reports directly to Dan Lahey, who holds budget authority; Rod would need to secure that budget. Rod handed day-to-day ownership to Cory Griffiths in Q1 2025 (Next_Step__c narrative), and since then the buyer's internal process has centered on drafting requirements and an RFP meant to determine vendor shortlisting (Next_Step__c, 24 Oct 2025). That RFP has slipped three self-imposed deadlines (end of Oct 2025, Dec 2025, mid-Mar 2026) without landing, so the practical process and sign-off gates remain unconfirmed despite the known reporting line.

decision_criteria.narrative: The clearest documented criterion is the buyer's preference for one full-suite S2P vendor over multiple point solutions, spanning procurement, sourcing, spend analytics, contracts, supplier management and project/savings management (Next_Step__c narrative, ~Apr 2025). Formal criteria were meant to be captured in an internal RFP, first promised for end of October 2025 (Next_Step__c, 24 Oct 2025 entry) and never delivered, so no structured requirements document has actually reached Zycus. The 11 Sep 2025 demo notes show the buyer probing specific areas (ERP integrations with Oracle/SAP/Dynamics/Salesforce, spend analytics dashboards, QA/review workflows), hinting at real evaluation interests, but these were exploratory questions, not a stated scoring rubric.

Five active competitors (Coupa, iValua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba, iCertis) plus incumbent DDSI, no competitive discovery since Sept 2025 demo — Zycus competitive standing unknown

No specific evidence surfaces Coupa, Ivalua, JAGGAER, SAP Ariba or Icertis as actively ahead; the RFP that would clarify the field has been promised three times since Oct 2025 and never delivered.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: Zycus has genuine full-suite fit and a well-received September 2025 demo, but the deal has sat in a pre-RFP holding pattern for over four months, has never reached the named economic buyer, and carries a forecast (Best Case, 49 days to close) that the evidence does not support.

deal_risk.text: Executive alignment gap: CFO Dan Lahey (economic buyer) and Elisa Baptiste (SVP COO, contact role) have no documented engagement; the deal runs entirely through operational contacts (Cory Griffiths, Brian Lambert) who have each gone quiet for extended stretches.
Brian Lambert's email showed a stale January out-of-office auto-reply resurfacing in June 2026, raising doubt about whether he is actively monitoring this channel.
A Best Case forecast with a 49-day close, no RFP in hand, no economic-buyer engagement, and a buyer-stated 2-3 year replacement horizon is not defensible as recorded.

win_position.text: Full-suite S2P interest was confirmed by the buyer as far back as 2 Apr 2025 (single-vendor preference across procurement, sourcing, analytics, contracts, supplier management), matched by an extensive 11 Sep 2025 demo covering all core modules plus Merlin AI-driven autonomous sourcing.
Peer references (Brookdale, Benchmark) in senior living resonated with the buyer per the rep's Apr 2025 summary, giving Zycus a credible in-category proof point.

The named economic buyer, CFO Dan Lahey, has never been directly engaged; budget sign-off depends entirely on Rod Copple, who has gone largely quiet since handing procurement to Cory Griffiths around Q1 2025.
The buyer's internal RFP, meant to determine vendor shortlisting, has been promised and missed three times (end of Oct 2025, Dec 2025, mid-Mar 2026) with no confirmed status since.
On 9 Jun 2025 the buyer described their P2P replacement horizon as '2-3 years out,' directly at odds with the current 27 Aug 2026 close date, 49 days from today.

deal_momentum.text: The deal owner reached out again on 30 Jun 2026 (~9 days ago) to re-engage after the long RFP silence, showing the account has not been abandoned.
The last confirmed two-way buyer engagement (an incoming email reply) was 10 Nov 2025, roughly eight months ago; the only further documented buyer-side contact since is Brian Lambert on 26 Dec 2025.
Next Step has read 'Awaiting RFP' since 16 Mar 2026 (~4 months ago) with no dated forward milestone since.
No commercial, pricing, or legal conversation has occurred at any point in the record; the deal is still pre-RFP roughly 15 months after being created (28 Aug 2024).

customer_commitment.text: No buyer-voiced preference for Zycus over any named competitor exists in the evidence read; the strongest read is the rep's own characterization that the Sept 2025 demo was 'received well.'
Budget (~$150K+ SaaS, plus services) was only ever verbally confirmed by the buying team around Apr 2025 and has never been revalidated against the current $195,000 amount or with the CFO.
